title: Opening Keynote, Michael Truell | Compile 26
summary: Cursor announced three major releases at Compile 26: Cursor Mobile for mobile IDE access, Origin an agent-native Git platform, and a new proprietary model trained from scratch. The company reported that 95% of current usage is agent-driven and emphasized platform expansion beyond IDE integration.
writeup: The Compile 26 opening keynote, delivered by Michael Truell, covers three releases from Cursor: Cursor Mobile for mobile IDE access, Origin an agent-native Git platform, and a new proprietary model trained from scratch. The announcements signal Cursor's expansion beyond IDE integration, with the company reporting that 95% of current usage is agent-driven. The takeaways are the shift toward agent-centric workflows and a product platform that now spans mobile, version control, and an in-house model.
